**Input:**

- explanation: `Normalize character list response payloads to map alternate backend name fields into characterName so names render reliably.`
- input:

```
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: d:\at-template\at1\laughing-octo-journey\src\app\page\character\character-list.ts
@@
 export default class CharacterListPage implements OnDestroy {
@@
 	loadCharacters(): void {
@@
 		this.unsubscribeResponse = this.socketService.on(
 			CHARACTER_LIST_RESPONSE_EVENT,
 			(response: CharacterListResponse) => {
 				this.isLoading.set(false);
 				if (response.success) {
-					this.characters.set(response.characters ?? []);
+					this.characters.set(this.normalizeCharacters(response.characters));
 				} else {
 					this.characters.set([]);
 					this.errorMessage.set(response.message);
 				}
 				this.unsubscribeResponse?.();
 			},
 		);
@@
 		const request: CharacterListRequest = { playerName, sessionKey: this.sessionService.getSessionKey()! };
 		this.socketService.emit(CHARACTER_LIST_REQUEST_EVENT, request);
 	}
+
+	private normalizeCharacters(characters: unknown): PlayerCharacterSummary[] {
+		if (!Array.isArray(characters)) {
+			return [];
+		}
+
+		return characters.map((raw, index) => {
+			const item = (raw ?? {}) as {
+				id?: unknown;
+				characterId?: unknown;
+				characterName?: unknown;
+				name?: unknown;
+				character?: { name?: unknown };
+				level?: unknown;
+				createdAt?: unknown;
+			};
+
+			const nameFromObject =
+				typeof item.character?.name === 'string' ? item.character.name : undefined;
+			const resolvedCharacterName =
+				typeof item.characterName === 'string'
+					? item.characterName
+					: typeof item.name === 'string'
+						? item.name
+						: nameFromObject;
+
+			return {
+				id:
+					typeof item.id === 'string'
+						? item.id
+						: typeof item.characterId === 'string'
+							? item.characterId
+							: `char-${index}`,
+				characterName: (resolvedCharacterName ?? '').trim(),
+				level: typeof item.level === 'number' ? item.level : undefined,
+				createdAt: typeof item.createdAt === 'string' ? item.createdAt : undefined,
+			};
+		});
+	}
*** End Patch
```


</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="replace_string_in_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- filePath: `d:\at-template\at1\laughing-octo-journey\src\app\app.component.ts`
- newString:

```
  /**
   * Navigate to a route in the primary outlet (canvas/scene).
   * Optionally activate a left-panel or right-panel route at the same time.
   * When rightRoute is omitted the right outlet is cleared so the canvas shows.
   */
  navigateTo(primaryRoute: string, leftRoute?: string, rightRoute?: string) {
    const outlets: Record<string, string[] | null> = {
      right: rightRoute ? [rightRoute] : null,
    };
    if (leftRoute) {
      outlets['left'] = [leftRoute];
    }
    this.router.navigate([primaryRoute, { outlets }], { preserveFragment: true });
  }
```

- oldString:

```
  /**
   * Navigate to a route in the primary outlet (right panel)
   * Optionally navigate to a route in the left panel using auxiliary outlet
   */
  navigateTo(primaryRoute: string, leftRoute?: string) {
    const commands: any[] = [primaryRoute];
    if (leftRoute) {
      commands.push({ outlets: { left: [leftRoute] } });
    }
    this.router.navigate(commands);
  }
```


</details></tool-use>

intro, knot and scene-graph links at the are not switching to canvas when clicked

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The problem is the navigate call mixes a positional primary segment with an outlets object — Angular doesn't reconcile those correctly. The fix is to drive all three outlets through a single `{ outlets }` object.
</details></think>

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="replace_string_in_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- filePath: `d:\at-template\at1\laughing-octo-journey\src\app\app.component.ts`
- newString:

```
  /**
   * Navigate to a route in the primary outlet (canvas/scene).
   * Optionally activate a left-panel or right-panel route at the same time.
   * When rightRoute is omitted the right outlet is cleared so the canvas shows.
   */
  navigateTo(primaryRoute: string, leftRoute?: string, rightRoute?: string) {
    const outlets: Record<string, string[] | null> = {
      primary: [primaryRoute],
      right: rightRoute ? [rightRoute] : null,
    };
    if (leftRoute) {
      outlets['left'] = [leftRoute];
    }
    this.router.navigate([{ outlets }], { preserveFragment: true });
  }
```

- oldString:

```
  /**
   * Navigate to a route in the primary outlet (canvas/scene).
   * Optionally activate a left-panel or right-panel route at the same time.
   * When rightRoute is omitted the right outlet is cleared so the canvas shows.
   */
  navigateTo(primaryRoute: string, leftRoute?: string, rightRoute?: string) {
    const outlets: Record<string, string[] | null> = {
      right: rightRoute ? [rightRoute] : null,
    };
    if (leftRoute) {
      outlets['left'] = [leftRoute];
    }
    this.router.navigate([primaryRoute, { outlets }], { preserveFragment: true });
  }
```


</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

Let me look at the current navigateTo implementation and how the routes are structured. The issue is likely that passing `null` for the right outlet in the same navigation as a primary route isn't working correctly.

Let me check the current state of the navigate call and think about Angular router URL structure.

The routes are:
- Primary routes like `knot`, `intro`, `scene-graph` are top-level
- Left outlet routes like `left:intro`, `left:login` etc.
- Right outlet routes like `right:drone-view-specs`

The navigateTo currently does:
```typescript
this.router.navigate([primaryRoute, { outlets }], { preserveFragment: true });


```

But I'm realizing the issue—you can't mix a primary route with outlet commands in the same navigate call. The outlets object applies to secondary outlets relative to that segment, not the primary route itself. I need to structure this differently, probably by separating the primary route navigation from the outlet clearing.

```

typescript
this.router.navigate(['knot', { outlets: { right: null } }], { preserveFragment: true });
```

This would navigate to something weird like `/knot(right:)` or might fail.

The proper way in Angular to clear a named outlet while navigating to primary is:
```typescript
this.router.navigate([{ outlets: { primary: ['knot'], right: null } }], { preserveFragment: true });
```

Let me reconsider. The original working code was:
```typescript
const commands: any[] = [primaryRoute];
if (leftRoute) {
  commands.push({ outlets: { left: [leftRoute] } });
}
this.router.navigate(commands);
```

This worked for canvas switching. The problem now is we need to also clear the right outlet.

One approach: separate the navigation into two steps

---

I'm realizing the original approach of building up the commands array worked well for the canvas switching, but now I need to handle clearing the right outlet at the same time. The tricky part is getting the outlet syntax right - I could try combining the primary route with an explicit null for the right outlet in a single navigate call, or I might need to split this into separate navigation steps to ensure the right outlet actually gets cleared.

Let me think through whether this would break the existing nav link behavior where we're just passing simple route names like 'intro' or 'knot' - I need to verify that the new outlets-based approach is backward compatible with those simpler calls.

The current broken code tries to mix a route segment with an outlets specification, which Angular doesn't handle correctly for primary routes. The fix is to restructure the navigateTo method to always use the outlets object, specifying the primary route and clearing the right outlet, with optional left and right routes.

Updating the method now...
